summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the series 'God of the Valleys' with Part Two, focusing on how God works in our difficult seasons. He began by acknowledging that life is full of valleys—times of loss, sickness, loneliness, and failure—and that Jesus never promised a valley-free life. However, God remains faithful and is still God in the valleys. Pastor Travis used Psalm 23 as the foundation, explaining that valleys have a purpose in God's plan. He offered four key takeaways for when we are in the valley: God is growing us, God is defending us, God is anointing us, and God is setting us up for a blessing. He emphasized that trials produce perseverance and maturity, and that we can trust God to fight our battles for us, as illustrated by David and Goliath and the parting of the Red Sea. The message concluded with a call to trust God's process, even in crushing circumstances, believing that He will turn our shortages into a surplus of blessing. An invitation was given for those in need of prayer or wanting to surrender their lives to Jesus.
mainPoints: When we are in the valleys, God is growing us, using trials to produce perseverance and spiritual maturity.
God is our defender; we do not need to fight our own battles but can trust Him to protect and vindicate us.
God is anointing and blessing us through our valleys, using the crushing experiences to set us apart for His purpose and future abundance.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.

Dear friends, do not be surprised at the fiery ordeal that has come on you to test you, as though something strange were happening to you. But rejoice inasmuch as you participate in the sufferings of Christ, so that you may be overjoyed when his glory is revealed.

The man of God came up and told the king of Israel, 'This is what the Lord says: "Because the Arameans think the Lord is a god of the hills and not a god of the valleys, I will deliver this vast army into your hands, and you will know that I am the Lord."'

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.

These have come so that the proven genuineness of your faith—of greater worth than gold, which perishes even though refined by fire—may result in praise, glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.

The Spirit of the Sovereign Lord is on me, because the Lord has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives and release from darkness for the prisoners.

The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free.
But he was pierced for our transgressions, he was crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was on him, and by his wounds we are healed.
You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives.
And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:1-3 - 'The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing... he refreshes my soul.'
Monday.action: Identify one area of need or anxiety today and consciously choose to trust God as your provider.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for being my shepherd. I trust you to lead and provide for me today, especially in [mention your need]. Refresh my soul with your peace.

Tuesday.scripture: James 1:2-4 - 'Consider it pure joy... whenever you face trials of many kinds...'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a current difficulty. Write down one way God might be using it to grow your character or faith.
Tuesday.prayer: God, help me see my trials through your eyes. Give me the grace to find joy in the process of becoming more like you.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 23:5 - 'You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies.'
Wednesday.action: Choose not to engage in an argument or defend yourself in a conflict today. Instead, pray and trust God with the outcome.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I choose to sit at your table of peace. I release my need to defend myself and trust you to fight for me.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 61:1 - 'The Spirit of the Sovereign Lord is on me, because the Lord has anointed me...'
Thursday.action: Think of a past painful experience. Ask God to show you how He can use that 'crushing' to help or encourage someone else.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, anoint me today. Take the difficult parts of my story and use them for good, to bring your hope to others.

Friday.scripture: Romans 8:28 - 'And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him...'
Friday.action: Declare out loud, 'God is working this for my good,' over a situation that currently feels like a loss or failure.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I choose to believe your promise. Even when I can't see it, I trust that you are working all things together for my good because I love you.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 23:6 - 'Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life...'
Saturday.action: Look ahead to the coming week. Write down one specific way you are expecting God's goodness and mercy to show up.
Saturday.prayer: God, I thank you that your goodness and mercy chase me down, even in the valleys. I open my heart to receive the blessings you have for me.
